lede: A look at September 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ziaire Williams and Sergio Parisse, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Siberian Light Aviation Flight 51
events.desc: Siberian Light Aviation Flight 51 crashes short of the runway at Kazachinskoye Airport, killing four.

events.title: Typhoon Maemi
events.desc: Typhoon Maemi, the strongest recorded typhoon to strike South Korea, made landfall near Busan.

events.title: Iraq War
events.desc: Iraq War: In Fallujah, U.S. forces mistakenly shoot and kill eight Iraqi police officers.

events.title: Wells Fargo
events.desc: A Wells Fargo depot in West Hartford, Connecticut, United States, is robbed of approximately US$7 million by Los Macheteros.

events.title: African and Malagasy Union
events.desc: The African and Malagasy Union is founded.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: RMS Laconia, carrying civilians, Allied soldiers and Italian POWs is torpedoed off the coast of West Africa and sinks with a heavy loss of life.

events.title: Leo Szilard
events.desc: Leó Szilárd, waiting for a red light on Southampton Row in Bloomsbury, conceives the idea of the nuclear chain reaction.

events.title: Southern Rhodesia
events.desc: Southern Rhodesia, today called Zimbabwe, is annexed by the United Kingdom.

events.title: Gustav Mahler
events.desc: Premiere performance of Gustav Mahler's Symphony No. 8 in Munich (with a chorus of 852 singers and an orchestra of 171 players. Mahler's rehearsal assistant conductor was Bruno Walter).

events.title: Battle of North Point
events.desc: Battle of North Point: an American detachment halts the British land advance to Baltimore in the War of 1812.

deaths.text: Sitaram Yechury, Indian politician and leader of CPI(M)
Tom Sims, American skateboarder and snowboarder, founded Sims Snowboards
Jan van der Heyden, Dutch painter and illustrator

observances.text: Christian feast day: Ailbe (Elvis, Eilfyw) of Emly
Christian feast day: The Most Holy Name of the Blessed Virgin Mary
Christian feast day: John Henry Hobart (Episcopal Church (USA))
Christian feast day: Laisrén mac Nad Froích
